query: I'm fitting a hierarchical logistic regression in PyMC. The data is 30 clinical trial sites with about 50 patients per site and a binary outcome. The default sampler reports 47 divergences out of 8000 draws. The site-level random-intercept SD has prior HalfNormal(100). Walk me through diagnosing and fixing this.
expected_behavior: Identifies the HalfNormal(100) prior on the site-level SD as the likely root cause of the funnel divergences and recommends tightening it to a weakly-informative prior such as HalfNormal(1) or HalfStudentT(3, 0, 1)

Recommends a non-centered re-parameterization of the site intercepts (alpha[i] = mu + sigma * alpha_raw[i] with alpha_raw[i] ~ Normal(0, 1)) and/or raising target_accept to roughly 0.95 to handle the hierarchical funnel
Requires checking the diagnostic gate — r_hat, ESS bulk and tail, and divergences — and refuses to interpret the posterior until divergences are essentially zero and r_hat is at or below 1.01
